question: Based on the stacked bar chart showing quarterly performance indicators for Store X, what can we conclude about which quarter has the best overall performance?

options.A: Q4 has the best overall performance because it has the highest combined bar height.
options.B: Q2 has the best overall performance because it has the highest store visits.
options.C: Q1 has the best overall performance because it has the lowest electricity consumption.
options.D: We cannot determine the best overall performance from this chart because it stacks indicators with different units that cannot be meaningfully added.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Store Visits, Electricity Consumption, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
